project_name: Precision-Filtered Evidence Anchors for Distractor-Robust QA

decision.recommended_next_action: Finalize this branch as supported on the synthetic distractor benchmark; next validation, if desired, should test precision-filtered anchors on a less templated external QA corpus rather than branching this routing artifact again.
decision.stop_reason: The precision-filtered selector beat vanilla top-k at all tested budgets and beat vanilla on the previously failing code_direct paraphrase while using far fewer prompt tokens.

recommended_next_action: Finalize this branch as supported on the synthetic distractor benchmark; next validation, if desired, should test precision-filtered anchors on a less templated external QA corpus rather than branching this routing artifact again.
run_notes_tail: # Run Notes — Precision-Filtered Evidence Anchors for Distractor-Robust QA
## 2026-04-19 execution plan

1. Acquired the parent implementation and benchmark harness into this branch (`src/`, `tests/`, README, model symlink) because this successor directory initially contained only routing prompts and OMX state.

2. Define branch kill condition: precision-filtered anchors are non-robust if the same Qwen2.5 0.5B generative sweep over budgets 140/180/220 and original/code_direct/semantic paraphrases fails to beat vanilla top-k at every budget, or still loses materially on the code_direct paraphrase.

3. Implement a distinct selector that suppresses distractor/provisional/negated value sentences before anchor packing and only admits value-bearing sentences with final-review/assignment evidence cues.
4. Add regression tests for distractor suppression and budget compliance, then run the offline retention sweep.

5. If local llama.cpp/model are available, rerun the same 24-item generative benchmark comparing vanilla, parent anchored span packing, and precision-filtered anchors; otherwise leave a concrete `continue` decision pointing to that exact benchmark command.
## 2026-04-19 completed work
- Copied parent source/tests and linked the parent Qwen2.5 0.5B GGUF model into this branch.
- Implemented `precision_filtered_anchor_pack` plus reusable distractor detection/redaction helpers in `src/evidence_anchor_packer.py`.
- Updated `src/generative_qa_benchmark.py` to evaluate vanilla, parent anchors, and precision-filtered anchors side-by-side.
- Added regression coverage for provisional-value distractor suppression and precision packer budget/retention behavior.
- Verification:
- `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q` -> 7 passed.
- Offline 120-item sweep at budgets 140/180/220 wrote `results/offline_precision_filtered/` and preserved answer/evidence retention while using ~48-53 prompt tokens.
- Qwen2.5 0.5B generative sweep wrote `results/generative_qwen_24_precision_filtered/`.

- Key generative result: precision-filtered anchors beat vanilla at every budget (F1 0.7778 vs 0.0069 at 140; 0.7500 vs 0.3750 at 180; 0.7778 vs 0.7292 at 220), and beat vanilla on code_direct paraphrases (0.6111 vs 0.4514). This clears the branch kill condition.

- Performance notes: full generative run completed in 23.30s wall time under `/usr/bin/time -v`, max RSS 969800 KB; mean per-request model latency for precision-filtered anchors was 26.29ms/21.91ms/22.67ms at budgets 140/180/220.
## Interpretation

The parent failure was not evidence retention: it was low-precision exposure of competing answer-shaped values. Precision filtering is supported on this synthetic distractor benchmark because it removes those copyable distractors and keeps only the final reviewed value claim.
# Run Notes — Precision-Filtered Evidence Anchors for Distractor-Robust QA
## 2026-04-19 execution plan

1. Acquired the parent implementation and benchmark harness into this branch (`src/`, `tests/`, README, model symlink) because this successor directory initially contained only routing prompts and OMX state.

2. Define branch kill condition: precision-filtered anchors are non-robust if the same Qwen2.5 0.5B generative sweep over budgets 140/180/220 and original/code_direct/semantic paraphrases fails to beat vanilla top-k at every budget, or still loses materially on the code_direct paraphrase.

3. Implement a distinct selector that suppresses distractor/provisional/negated value sentences before anchor packing and only admits value-bearing sentences with final-review/assignment evidence cues.
4. Add regression tests for distractor suppression and budget compliance, then run the offline retention sweep.

5. If local llama.cpp/model are available, rerun the same 24-item generative benchmark comparing vanilla, parent anchored span packing, and precision-filtered anchors; otherwise leave a concrete `continue` decision pointing to that exact benchmark command.
## 2026-04-19 completed work
- Copied parent source/tests and linked the parent Qwen2.5 0.5B GGUF model into this branch.
- Implemented `precision_filtered_anchor_pack` plus reusable distractor detection/redaction helpers in `src/evidence_anchor_packer.py`.
- Updated `src/generative_qa_benchmark.py` to evaluate vanilla, parent anchors, and precision-filtered anchors side-by-side.
- Added regression coverage for provisional-value distractor suppression and precision packer budget/retention behavior.
- Verification:
- `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q` -> 7 passed.
- Offline 120-item sweep at budgets 140/180/220 wrote `results/offline_precision_filtered/` and preserved answer/evidence retention while using ~48-53 prompt tokens.
- Qwen2.5 0.5B generative sweep wrote `results/generative_qwen_24_precision_filtered/`.

- Key generative result: precision-filtered anchors beat vanilla at every budget (F1 0.7778 vs 0.0069 at 140; 0.7500 vs 0.3750 at 180; 0.7778 vs 0.7292 at 220), and beat vanilla on code_direct paraphrases (0.6111 vs 0.4514). This clears the branch kill condition.

- Performance notes: full generative run completed in 23.30s wall time under `/usr/bin/time -v`, max RSS 969800 KB; mean per-request model latency for precision-filtered anchors was 26.29ms/21.91ms/22.67ms at budgets 140/180/220.
## Interpretation

The parent failure was not evidence retention: it was low-precision exposure of competing answer-shaped values. Precision filtering is supported on this synthetic distractor benchmark because it removes those copyable distractors and keeps only the final reviewed value claim.
